title: pseudoselago subglabra
intro: Pseudoselago subglabra, also known as the 'False Selago', is a plant species that belongs to the family of flowering plants, Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Pseudoselago subglabra has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Pseudoselago subglabra can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal values.
